topic: Structural completeness — does Part 2b properly reference the strongest examples? Do the counts in prose match the tag data?
change_summary: Last HOL-COMPLETENESS review was 2026-04-06 (8 days ago). V6 restructure, many churn-and-burn queue items today, and multiple tag corrections have occurred since. Re-running to catch Part 2b prose/tag drift.
scope: Full audit of data/sections.json part2b vs. data/wins.json code_analysis tags. Verified every stratification count, every named-WIN list, and every hardcoded number in part2b against the current 70-WIN wins.json.
our_argument_summary: Part 2b argues that the dome's code-level structural signature (absent/hardcoded monitoring, relabeling standard physics, post-hoc adoption, no derivation from dome geometry) invalidates the '69 confirmed predictions' framing. Section 2b uses a mix of build-time templates ({{CA_NONE}}, {{CA_HARDCODED}}, {{CA_LIVE}}, {{CA_RELABELS}}, {{CA_POSTHOC}}, {{CA_DOME}}, {{TOTAL_WINS}}, {{CA_REVIEWED}}, etc.) and hardcoded numbers to express these counts.

holes_found.location: Section 2b.3, paragraph beginning 'The derivation question (the central issue)'
holes_found.description: Internal contradiction after template expansion. The sentence reads 'Of the {{CA_REVIEWED}} WINs reviewed, only {{CA_DOME}} derive their predicted values from the dome's own geometric equations. These four are: WIN-020, WIN-044, WIN-057, WIN-058.' After build-time expansion, {{CA_DOME}} renders as '2' but the very next clause says 'These four are'. Readers see 'only 2 derive ... These four are: WIN-020, WIN-044, WIN-057, WIN-058' — a visible arithmetic contradiction that a dome defender can trivially cite as 'your own review cannot count.'
holes_found.evidence: Current data/wins.json: derives_from_dome=true for only IDs 044 and 057. WIN-020 has derives_from_dome=false (matching the prose's own argument that gear ratios are fitted, not derived). WIN-058 has derives_from_dome=false (matching the prose's own argument that 0.9941 is a regression coefficient). The 'these four are' list is stale — it predates the tag corrections that brought the per-WIN metadata into agreement with the argumentative text.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite the sentence to: 'Of the {{CA_REVIEWED}} WINs reviewed, only {{CA_DOME}} derive their predicted values from the dome's own geometric equations — WIN-044 (Firmament Scaling Function) and WIN-057 (two-zone disc topology).' Then discuss WIN-020 and WIN-058 in a separate 'near-miss candidates' paragraph that explicitly notes they were previously cited as derivations but fail the test on scrutiny. Consider extending build.js to emit a named list of derives_from_dome=true WIN IDs so this list cannot drift again.

holes_found.location: Section 2b.3, paragraph beginning 'The four exceptions deserve individual scrutiny'
holes_found.description: WIN-020 is described as 'the one geometrically-derived prediction that has been empirically confirmed,' but (a) its current tag says derives_from_dome=false, (b) its current verdict is 'Std Model Explains' (not a confirmed dome derivation), and (c) the prose itself concludes three sentences later that 'the gear ratios are freely chosen, not derived from the disc radius or firmament height.' The framing contradicts its own subsequent argument.
holes_found.recommendation: Either drop WIN-020 from the named exceptions list entirely (supported by current tags) or keep it as a 'was cited as a derivation but fails on inspection' example. Do not describe it as 'empirically confirmed' — it is Std Model Explains in the current taxonomy.

holes_found.description: WIN-044 is described as 'pending verification against the August 2026 eclipse,' but WIN-044's current verdict is Self-Contradicted. The coordinate-collapse restructure (Section 1.8 → EXP-149) changed WIN-044's status from pending to self-contradicted, but Part 2b was not updated. A dome defender reading this will note that Part 2b and Part 1.8 disagree about WIN-044's status.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite to: 'WIN-044 (Firmament Scaling Function) is the dome's strongest candidate for a geometric derivation — the formula is explicitly constructed from dome height parameters — but Section 1.8 shows the derivation inherits the coordinate-system contamination from Polaris-calibrated H(r), so WIN-044 is Self-Contradicted even though its tag derives_from_dome=true.' This turns the apparent tension into a cross-section reinforcement.

holes_found.location: Section 2b.3, sentence '43 — nearly two-thirds — fail all four tests simultaneously. An additional 19 WINs fail three of the four tests. Only 5 WINs avoid all four structural failures'
holes_found.description: The '43', '19', and '5' are hardcoded from an older 67-WIN baseline. Current cross-tabulation on 70 WINs yields 47 quadruple-failures (+4), 18 triple-failures (−1), and 5 low-failure (unchanged coincidentally because 70−47−18=5 and 67−43−19=5). 'Nearly two-thirds' still holds (47/70 ≈ 67%). But the specific '43' is now wrong, and the review's own CLAUDE.md rule says 'Never hardcode a number that can be derived from the data.'
holes_found.recommendation: Template these three counts: {{CA_QUAD_FAIL}}, {{CA_TRIPLE_FAIL}}, {{CA_LOW_FAIL}} computed in build.js from the same code_analysis cross-tabulation. This makes the numbers self-correcting and removes a surface a dome defender can point at to say 'you criticize my hardcoded 94.5% but you hardcode your own 43.'

holes_found.location: Section 2b.3, sentences 'Instead, 63 of 67 WINs work backwards' and 'Zero of 67 WINs meet all four criteria. This is the single most important finding of the entire code analysis.'
holes_found.description: The '67' baseline is stale. Current TOTAL_WINS=70. A headline sentence that calls itself 'the single most important finding of the entire code analysis' should not use a drift-prone number. The companion 'Zero' is still true (no WIN today satisfies derives_from_dome=true AND post_hoc=false AND relabels_standard=false AND monitoring=live_fetch — verified empty set), but the '67' must become '70' or '{{TOTAL_WINS}}'.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace '67' with '{{TOTAL_WINS}}' in both sentences. Also replace the '63' with an explicit computed value (currently 68: 70 − CA_DOME=2 = 68 'working backwards'; but 63 appears to have been computed differently in the original — recheck build.js for the source formula). Add a build-time assertion in test.js: 'Zero WINs simultaneously satisfy derives_from_dome=true, post_hoc=false, relabels_standard=false, monitoring=live_fetch' — so a future tag change that breaks this claim fails the test instead of silently contradicting the prose.

holes_found.location: Section 2b.3, sentence 'The 43 quadruple-failure WINs include every tidal constituent (WIN-045/046/049/050/051), every Schumann resonance claim (WIN-001/002/029/038/061), the majority of geomagnetic secular variation claims, and all Tesla/distance/heat claims.'
holes_found.description: The named lists are verifiable and correct against the current quadruple-failure set — all 5 tidal and all 5 Schumann WINs are in the current 47-WIN quad list. But the leading '43' ties this claim to a stale count. When the '43' is templated (per the prior hole), keep this sentence intact — the named lists are stable.
holes_found.recommendation: No change to the named lists. When fixing the '43' count, leave the 'every tidal constituent' and 'every Schumann resonance claim' clauses intact. Consider adding a test.js invariant: 'All tidal WINs (045, 046, 049, 050, 051) are in the quadruple-failure set' so a future tag correction does not silently invalidate this example.

holes_found.description: This is historical V51.1 arithmetic describing the dome's own published claim at that version. It sits immediately next to our computed '85.2%' derived from 69/(69+4+8). The 69 is V51.1-era and will become confusing once the dome ships V52 with a different WIN count. Reader cognitive load: two '69' numbers that are the dome's version-specific count, adjacent to templated current counts, invites 'which 69 is which.'
holes_found.recommendation: Minor edit to add a parenthetical clarifying the 69 refers to the dome's V51.1 self-count (as opposed to our {{TOTAL_WINS}}=70 review coverage). Example: 'e.g., 69/(69+4) = 94.5% in V51.1 (the dome counted 69 WINs at that version; we currently review {{TOTAL_WINS}}).'

holes_found.location: Section 2b.5, sentence 'The claim_index.json indexes only 9 of 69 confirmed claims'
holes_found.description: The '69' here is the dome's own claim count in its claim_index.json — not our review count. This is a factual quote from the dome infrastructure. Worth confirming with the poller that the current dome claim_index.json still indexes 9 and still totals 69 (the dome may have migrated to a different count).
holes_found.recommendation: Add a data/social:evidence item confirming the current claim_index.json indexing ratio, or have the poller flag any future change to the '9 of 69' ratio. If the dome moves to 9 of 70 (or any other ratio), the prose number needs updating.

holes_found.description: Part 2b cross-tabulates the four structural tests and names quadruple-failure WINs, but it does NOT cross-link to Section 1.8 (Coordinate Collapse) or Section 1.9 (Differential Refraction) — arguably our two strongest kill-shot arguments. A reader of Part 2b learns 'most WINs are structurally problematic' but does not learn 'the coordinate system itself is self-contradicted,' which is a more devastating finding.
holes_found.recommendation: Add one paragraph at the end of Section 2b.3 or as a new Section 2b.4 bridge: 'The four-failure pattern documented above is structural — it shows that the dome's predictions are, by code-level construction, not tests. Section 1.8 (The Coordinate Collapse) supplies the complementary geometric finding: the dome's coordinate system itself is derived through the same aetheric medium the refraction fix requires, so every WIN that depends on Polaris-calibrated distances (which is most of them) is contaminated at input. The structural signature and the geometric collapse reinforce: the dome's code and the dome's geometry fail independently and for different reasons.'

holes_found.location: Section 2b.3, sentence 'Only 5 WINs avoid all four structural failures'
holes_found.description: The '5' is currently numerically correct (70 − 47 − 18 = 5), but for a completely different set of WINs than when written. Current low-failure WINs: 007, 024, 031, 044, 057. The prose's qualifier 'and even those are problematic for other reasons' still holds — all 5 have problematic verdicts (Misleading/Unfalsifiable/Self-Contradicted).
holes_found.recommendation: Template the 5 as {{CA_LOW_FAIL}}. Optionally include the named list after expansion: 'Only {{CA_LOW_FAIL}} WINs (007, 024, 031, 044, 057) avoid all four structural failures, and even those are Misleading, Unfalsifiable, or Self-Contradicted on other grounds.'

holes_found.location: Section 2b.1, sentence 'Over half the dome's "confirmed predictions" are not monitored by any mechanism.'
holes_found.description: 41/70 = 58.6% — still 'over half,' but barely. If CA_NONE drops below 50% in a future cycle, 'over half' becomes wrong without any other number changing.
holes_found.recommendation: Either keep as-is (currently correct) or change to 'A majority of the dome's "confirmed predictions" are not monitored by any mechanism.' Low priority.

stronger_arguments.description: Part 2b currently treats the four structural tests as independent features. A stronger framing is that they are NOT independent: relabeling standard physics creates the opportunity to be post-hoc (you relabel because the standard-physics answer is known); post-hoc is what makes hardcoded monitoring safe (there is no future data to betray you); not-deriving-from-dome is what makes relabeling possible. The four tests are mutually reinforcing failure modes of the same underlying problem. A dome defender could object that four correlated tests are one test in disguise; our response is that the correlation IS the pattern: a scientific model would produce predictions where at least one dimension (novel derivation, novel observation, live testing, novel observable) showed independence. The dome produces zero.

Section 2b.7 is a strong kill-shot (the results.json 35/35 = 100% with 91% null-value entries) but is currently positioned as the LAST subsection. Readers who scan the document navigation and click only the first few subsections will miss it. Consider promoting 2b.7 earlier or pulling a TLDR of it into 2b.1. The '100% pass rate is mathematically unreachable' finding is the single cleanest demonstration that monitoring is not validation.

kernel_of_truth.description: Part 2b does correctly use templates for the primary counts (monitoring, relabels, post_hoc, dome_derivation, verdict tallies, total_wins, reviewed). The stratification in Section 2b.1 (2+7+15+12+3+2=41 = CA_NONE) is internally consistent with the current tag data. The named examples (Schumann WIN-002, tidal WIN-045/046/049/050/051, Antarctic WIN-067) are correct and their inclusion in the quadruple-failure list is verified. The build system did the right thing for the top-level aggregates.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The holes above are not in the aggregates — they are in the places where prose authors hardcoded a number instead of using a template (43, 19, 5, 67), or named a list of example WINs that subsequent tag corrections have invalidated (WIN-020, WIN-058 as derives-from-dome). The discipline applied to top-level counts was not uniformly applied to mid-paragraph numbers and named lists. This is exactly the pattern CLAUDE.md warns against: 'Never hardcode a number that can be derived from the data — we criticize the dome model for doing exactly that.'

recommended_action_details: Section 2b.3 needs a targeted rewrite for the 'These four are' framing and the hardcoded 43/19/67 counts. Section 2b.1's V51.1 parenthetical needs a clarifying edit. A new bridge paragraph (2b.4 or end of 2b.3) should link to Section 1.8 coordinate collapse. This is not a structural restructure — the argument is correct, the counts need templating and the named lists need updating.
deeper_analysis: The most damning finding is hole #1 — after template expansion, a reader can SEE 'only 2 derive... These four are.' The dome author, if he reads our review, will find this immediately. It is the exact same category of error we criticize him for (hardcoded numbers that contradict derived counts on the same page). The fix is mechanical: update the named list to {WIN-044, WIN-057} and reframe WIN-020/WIN-058 as 'previously cited, now known to not derive.' The deeper finding is that CLAUDE.md's 'Never hardcode a number' rule is not enforced by any test. A test.js invariant per templated count, and a linter that flags bare numbers next to template placeholders, would prevent this class of drift. The HOL-COMPLETENESS check has now caught four instances (43, 19, 67, and the derives-from-dome list); enforcing the rule automatically would eliminate the need for holistic re-review of this specific dimension.

Section 2b.1: 'other seven (WIN-002/012/029/038/045/046/049) do have monitoring domains' — matches Self-Contradicted intersect monitoring=hardcoded
Section 2b.3: tidal (WIN-045/046/049/050/051) all in quadruple-failure set — verified
Section 2b.3: Schumann (WIN-001/002/029/038/061) all in quadruple-failure set — verified
citations_failed: Section 2b.3: 'These four are: WIN-020, WIN-044, WIN-057, WIN-058' — only 044 and 057 have derives_from_dome=true in current tags
Section 2b.3: '43 quadruple-failure WINs' — current count is 47
Section 2b.3: 'An additional 19 WINs fail three' — current count is 18
Section 2b.3: '63 of 67 WINs work backwards' — 67 baseline is stale (current 70); need to recheck what 63 was computing
Section 2b.3: 'Zero of 67 WINs meet all four criteria' — 67 stale; 'Zero' still true but '67' should be '70' or templated
Section 2b.3: WIN-044 'pending verification against the August 2026 eclipse' — WIN-044 verdict is now Self-Contradicted

Section 2b.4: 'monitor.py defines 39 monitoring domains' — external dome file at commit 8e4bf9b, historical claim, not re-verified this cycle
Section 2b.7: results.json endpoint content — captured pre-removal (2026-04-10), no current re-verification possible

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender who has read our six methodological principles would note: 'Your Section 2b cross-tabulates 70 WINs across four tests and reports specific integer counts (43, 19, 5). I recomputed and got 47, 18, 5. Your own homepage critiques me for presenting a hardcoded 94.5% that does not match computation from the source data. Your 43 is hardcoded and does not match computation from YOUR source data. This is not a minor accounting difference — it is the same category of error. Furthermore, in the same paragraph you say "only 2 derive" and "these four are." Pick a number. If your review cannot maintain internal arithmetic consistency, what credibility do your criticisms of my scorecard have?' This is a genuinely effective rebuttal — it converts our own methodological rule into an attack.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Fix the hardcoded numbers (43, 19, 67) by templating and fix the 'These four are' contradiction before any defender notices. Add test.js invariants so this class of drift is impossible. Publish a short note in the changes log acknowledging that we caught our own hardcoded-number drift via holistic review — this preemptively disarms the 'you do what you criticize' attack by documenting that our process self-corrects.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: The 'These four are' named list in Section 2b.3 is not compatible with current per-WIN tags: WIN-020 and WIN-058 have derives_from_dome=false in wins.json but are listed as derivations in part2b prose. WIN-044's 'pending verification' framing in Section 2b.3 is not compatible with its Self-Contradicted verdict in wins.json. No cross-WIN contradictions between individual WIN review arguments themselves — the inconsistencies are all prose-vs-tag, not WIN-vs-WIN.

claims_checked: Quadruple-failure count = 47 (monitoring in {none,hardcoded} AND relabels_standard AND post_hoc AND NOT derives_from_dome)
CA_NONE stratification: 2 Unfalsifiable + 7 Self-Contradicted + 15 Misleading + 12 Std Model Explains + 3 Refuted + 2 Not Demonstrated = 41
Self-Contradicted with monitoring=hardcoded = 7 (named set {002,012,029,038,045,046,049} matches prose)
Self-Contradicted with monitoring=none = 7 (named set {044,050,051,053,056,061,067} matches prose)
Live-fetch WINs = 4: {004, 006, 007, 024} (matches named set in prose)
quantitative_verification.computations: Used Node script against data/wins.json from fresh clone of funwithscience-org/dome-model-review at commit f38bd00 (post-pull 2026-04-14T18:48Z). Cross-tabulated monitoring, relabels_standard, post_hoc, derives_from_dome for all 70 WINs. Counted verdicts and named sets. Verified all stratification arithmetic.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Part 2b's '43' quadruple-failure count does not match recomputed 47. Part 2b's '19' triple-failure count does not match recomputed 18. Part 2b's 'These four are' list does not match recomputed derives_from_dome=true set {044, 057}. Part 2b's 67-WIN baseline does not match current 70. All other numerical claims verified.
